items.text: Gradient descent updates weights by moving in the direction of the negative gradient after backpropagation computes the needed partial derivatives.

messages.text: Why does the update rule use subtraction?

answer_source.fallback_detail: Gemini returned insufficient_evidence even though the retrieved lecture evidence supports a partial grounded explanation.

citations.snippet_text: Gradient descent updates weights by moving in the direction of the negative gradient.

reply_sections.heading: Grounded answer
reply_sections.text: The lecture ties the update step to moving opposite the gradient. In that framing, the subtraction sign marks a step away from the direction that would increase the loss, even though the slides focus more on computing gradients than on unpacking the minus sign symbol by symbol.

reply_sections.heading: External supplement
reply_sections.text: Supplementary background only: the uploaded materials do not fully cover 'Why does the update rule use subtraction'. Use external references only as background, and verify any broader explanation against your lecture slides or course textbook before relying on it.
